TextENGNew: To lure Teppei out of his house, we opened the door to a phone booth.
The phone booth stood alone at the edge of the forest, a short way down the paved road.
In the darkness of night, its fluorescent light flickered on and off, asserting its presence.
changeReason: Old says 'streetlight'; new correctly translates 蛍光灯 as 'fluorescent light.'

TextENGNew: What was a phone booth doing in a place like this?
I'd heard it was installed so that anyone who got lost in the forest or spotted a wildfire could call for help.
When I strained my ears, I thought I could hear someone's SOS.
The voice had to be Satoko's. The thought made my body burn.
I wanted to save Satoko as soon as possible.
That conviction never wavered, no matter how much anxiety welled up or how many doubts clung to me.
With every passing moment, it spread to every corner of my body, slowly overtaking everything, until at last it ruled me completely.
Fierce conviction robs you of thought and freedom alike.
changeReason: OLD personalizes as first-person past tense; NEW correctly renders as a general philosophical statement matching the Japanese.

TextENGNew: I had truly become a puppet.
I picked up the receiver to confirm the line was working, then closed the door for the time being.
Shion checked her wristwatch as she brushed the dried mud from its face.
``There's still time...``
We ducked into the shadow of the tall grass to await the fateful hour.
A silence that felt like eternity stretched out. The heavy weight of that quiet deepened the bond between us.
Our eyes never met. We simply waited in stillness. Carrying nothing but killing intent.
``Let's eat the lunch I brought.``
changeReason: OLD translates お弁当 as "dinner I made"; NEW correctly translates as "lunch I brought," changing the meal type.

TextENGNew: Rena broke the silence and produced the stacking boxes.
I had no appetite... Having her say something so out of touch at a time like this really took the wind out of my sails.
Well, that kind of otherworldly quality was very Rena, I supposed...
``You know the saying about empty stomachs, right?@k@vS18/02/160200297. We should refuel while we still can.``
changeReason: Old misinterprets proverb reference as personal statement; new correctly references the well-known saying.

TextENGNew: Rena pressed a pair of chopsticks into my hands, then matter-of-factly spread out a handkerchief and began opening the boxes.
``Might as well eat while we have the chance.``
Shion snapped her chopsticks apart and gracefully helped herself to a side dish.
``It's delicious!``
``Really~?@k@vS18/02/160200299. Hearing that from Shii-chan makes me so happy.``
``You got up early to make all of this.@k@vS18/06/160600303. Thank you, Rena-san.``
changeReason: Old mistranslates subject as 'We'; Shion is praising Rena ('You got up early').

TextENGNew: ``Ehehehe.``
Shion and Rena ate their food with contented expressions. I was dumbfounded by the sheer resilience of these women.
Watching the two of them absently, a memory surfaced.
Last year, a little bird Mom had been caring for died of illness.
She held its silent body close and cried for a long time.
I had no idea what to say, so I just paced aimlessly around her.
changeReason: Old says 'avoided going near her' but Japanese means pacing around her -- opposite actions.

TextENGNew: That was when a loud, utterly out-of-place growl broke the silence. It was Mom's stomach.
Mom laughed, embarrassed, then got straight to her feet and started making dinner without another word...
``Don't be silly.@k@vS18/00/162700037. You eat precisely when you don't feel like eating!@k@vS18/00/162700038. A person's strength comes from a full stomach!``
Mom's words echoed back to me now, as if to encourage me.
And with the lingering wish to perform at least one last act as a human being, I snapped my chopsticks apart.
``Here you go, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200302. Have some onigiri.``
The onigiri Rena offered was adorned with thin strips of egg and cherry blossom starch, its charming pattern a feast for the eyes.
But to me right now, it looked like nothing more than a massive lump of rice.
I managed to bring it close to my mouth, but my jaw refused to open.
changeReason: OLD implies voluntary stopping; NEW correctly conveys involuntary refusal (頑として受け付けようとはしない).

TextENGNew: ``Is it not to your liking...?``
``...That's not it at all.@k@vS18/01/160100442. It looks great. Thanks for the meal.``
``Of course, please enjoy.``
I pried my mouth open by force and crammed the onigiri in.
It was utterly tasteless, scentless. The only sensation was that of grinding sand between my teeth.
Nausea hit me several times, but I fought it down and chewed through.
``Keiichi-kun, does Rena's onigiri... taste bad?``
``No way, it's fine.@k@vS18/01/160100444. I'm just a little nervous, that's all.``
``Really?``
Not wanting to hurt Rena's feelings, I flashed her a V-sign.
``Thank goodness~``
Looking relieved, Rena reached into the box for another onigiri.
Wait, any more than this was genuinely impossible.
As if my silent prayer had been answered, the onigiri sailed past me and was offered to Shion instead.
``Please have some onigiri too, Shii-chan.``
``There's tuna inside.``
For some reason, Shion shook her head vigorously and glared at the onigiri.
``You don't like onigiri?``
``Is that made with canned tuna?@k@vS18/06/160600305. I can't eat that -- I absolutely despise canned food!``
Rena recoiled in surprise at the sudden rejection.
``I'm sorry, Rena-san.@k@vS18/06/160600307. I just really can't handle canned food...@k@vS18/06/160600308. I heard something awful about it once, and ever since...``
changeReason: NEW inserts content from the following line not in this entry's Japanese, while omitting the original meaning (目にするのも嫌なくらい).

TextENGNew: ``Well, a long time ago... I heard a terrible superstition about it.@k@vS18/06/160600310. ...I'm sorry, could you put it away, if you don't mind?``
changeReason: OLD translates 迷信 (superstition) as "rumor"; NEW correctly translates as "superstition."

TextENGNew: At Shion's words, Rena and I exchanged a look and fell silent.
And so we consumed our last supper in silence, mechanically sending it to our stomachs without bothering to taste it.
By the time we'd finished eating, we were already a few minutes past the scheduled call time.
Panicked that the plan might be going off the rails, we scrambled to the phone booth.
``Ahahaha...``
Rena let out a small laugh.
Her ability to laugh like normal even at a time like this felt incongruous, but I envied it too.
``It's like we're playing oshikura manju...``
The three of us had been trying to squeeze into the phone booth's cramped space all at once.
It was clearly over capacity. Rena and I ended up getting pushed out.
``...Hey, Satoko actually made it to the festival, right?``
``Yeah.@k@vS18/02/160200315. Rena asked Rika-chan to bring her along.``
``There's no chance Teppei stopped her from going?``
``He was reluctant, apparently, but he gave his approval.``
``I see... then we're good...``
Did even Teppei have a scrap of mercy left in him? Or maybe our desperate will had become a kind of telepathy, manipulating Teppei into compliance.
changeReason: Old says 'Teppei was all alone'; Japanese discusses whether Teppei had mercy and telepathic influence.

TextENGNew: I took our good fortune as an omen and held it close like a talisman.
``All right, I'll make the call.``
Shion fixed us with a resolute gaze and lifted the receiver.
She looked up at the ceiling for a moment, perhaps rehearsing the Houjou family's phone number in her head.
I held the door ajar with one hand and watched Shion absently through the glass.
The fluorescent light inside the booth refracted, making Shion's face look distorted.
Shion's slender fingers slowly turned the dial.
Before long, I could faintly hear the ringing tone.
That mechanical sound ensnared me.
As if my brain had gone absent. Everything around me lost its reality, leaving only me adrift.
I was forced to confront myself, whether I wanted to or not.
changeReason: Old says confronting Teppei; Japanese 自分自身に対峙 means forced self-confrontation.

TextENGNew: What I found when I reached inside was a savage desire to commit murder.
At the end of the long ringing, Teppei's sluggish voice answered.
``Is this the Houjou residence?``
Shion quietly lowered her eyes, fighting to stay calm.
``That's right, but... what's this about?``
``This is the Okinomiya Police Station.@k@vS18/06/160600314. Pardon me, but would this be Teppei Houjou-san?``
``Ah, y-yes.@k@vS18/21/162000054. ...That's right, but...``
At the mention of police, Teppei's voice turned conspicuously deferential. His small-mindedness was on full display -- and it was satisfying to hear.
``We currently have a Satoko Houjou-san in our protective custody.``
changeReason: Old reverses custody direction ('under your care'); Japanese means police have Satoko in protective custody.

TextENGNew: ``Whaaat? Satoko?!@k@vS18/21/162000056. What did that brat do...?!``
Completely unaware of the trap, Teppei was growing agitated.
Shion pressed on calmly, leaving no gap for suspicion to creep in.
``I'll explain the details once you arrive...@k@vS18/06/160600317. Could you come right away?``
``Right now...?@k@vS18/21/162000058. I was in the middle of a bath and I'm buck naked here...``
We already knew that. We'd researched Teppei's habit of bathing at this hour.
Right on schedule. The 'plan' was still on track! That certainty made my heart pound.
``Besides, I was about to go to bed, so heading out at this hour is kind of...``
Whether out of laziness or spite, Teppei was stringing together feeble excuses to avoid coming.
``I see...@k@vS18/06/160600319. If you're unable to come to us, we'll have to send someone to pick you up... if you don't mind.``
``Huh? What do you mean by that...?``
``The paperwork with the courts has already been filed.@k@vS18/06/160600322. First thing tomorrow morning--``
``Y-you mean a search warrant?!``
Shion's bluff sent Teppei's voice up an octave.
A moment's thought would tell anyone that the police would never give advance warning before executing a search warrant.
Whether he had something to hide from the police or was simply a coward cowed by authority, Teppei was panicking to an embarrassing degree.
``U-understood!@k@vS18/21/162000063. I'll head there right away, just please don't send a crowd to my door.``
``Very well. We'll be expecting you.``
*Click.*
Shion let out a deep sigh as she set the receiver down. Then she looked between us.
``......All right. The curtain rises.``
We looked at each other in silence, then nodded deeply.
Words were no longer necessary.
All that remained was to snuff out Teppei Houjou's life with my own hands...
We sprinted toward the Houjou house at full speed.
Since Shion had her scooter, she'd go ahead to set up at the scene while Rena and I followed.
We had some time -- Teppei would need to get dressed and probably have a cigarette -- but every second counted.
If Teppei somehow slipped past us and made it to town, everything was over. The entire plan would collapse back to square one.
That was something we had to prevent at all costs...!
My anxiety made me clumsy, and my feet slipped off the pedals several times.
Each jarring *thunk* frayed my nerves further.
...I stole the occasional glance behind me.
A short distance back, Rena was pedaling furiously, standing on her pedals.
The expression on her face was almost demonic.
Perhaps sensing my gaze, Rena's expression instantly shifted to an out-of-place smile.
The sense of mission -- to rescue a lone girl from the brink of despair. The obligation -- to keep showing that friendly smile to our classmates.
Caught between those two contradictory impulses, Rena's face was twisted.
Our 'rightful future.' For just a fleeting instant, a shadow fell across those words of overwhelming power.
Rushing to the scene at full speed... we passed several families wearing yukata along the way.
...Probably on their way to the festival.
...This was Hinamizawa -- even if I didn't recognize them, they might well know my face.
Just passing them by like this... sent a ripple of anxiety through my heart.
But this wasn't my usual neighborhood... so there was a chance they wouldn't recognize me.
Besides, just look at their faces.
Their heads were full of the Watanagashi, the once-a-year festival. Someone like me, who'd mastered the art of melting into shadow, wouldn't even register.
Enough. Worrying about it now was pointless.
I just had to reach the scene even one second sooner... catch my breath, and set the ambush...
``Kei-chan, over here.``
Shion, who'd already arrived at the scene, beckoned us over in a hushed voice.
``Teppei isn't here yet.@k@vS18/06/160600327. The preparations are done.``
Squinting into the darkness, I could see the jet-black rope stretched across the road like a slash.
I pulled Satoshi's bat from my backpack, wiped my clammy hands on my sleeve, and gripped it tight.
``Rena-san, please take the other end of the rope.``
``Yeah.``
Rena crouched low and slipped into the shadows on the opposite side of the road.
changeReason: OLD translates 身をかがめ as "bowed to Shion" (social gesture); NEW correctly translates as "crouched low" (physical concealment).

TextENGNew: Somewhere, the crickets' song seemed to jeer at me for brandishing the banner of justice while marching toward savagery.
Like a warning... or like applause...
Fire and water falling at once. A strange, contradictory balance.
*Rrring*...
The silence was torn apart with brutal, undeniable reality.
That sound -- the one from the wagon, the one from the bottom of the hole -- had come for me again, striking at my back.
I spun around, eyes wide.
There was Shion... holding the source of the noise in her hand.
``...Shion... what is that...?``
``...A stun gun.``
With a fearless smile, Shion held the stun gun aloft, letting its pale blue light flash.
*Rrring*...
With that ominous crackle, electricity arced and sparked in jagged lines.
``I felt bad about making you do all the killing alone, Kei-chan...@k@vS18/06/160600331. And besides... I was worried about whether you could really finish the job...``
My instincts told me her real reason was the latter.
In other words... she hadn't trusted me.
The whispered conversation I'd overheard from the bottom of the hole flashed back.
Doesn't know anything...
Going to die anyway...
Had the killing intent Shion had been quietly nurturing all this time been directed not only at Teppei... but at me as well...?
Why?!
Shion must have doubted my resolve to kill.
If I lost my nerve and tried to flee at the last moment, she would have killed me without hesitation.
Shion, who had insisted so strongly on eliminating all evidence.
Of course she had prepared a contingency for the worst-case scenario.
The realization of how far ahead Shion had been thinking -- how ruthlessly calculated she was -- made every hair on my body stand on end.
``I'm sorry for keeping it a secret...@k@vS18/06/160600333. I was afraid it might wound your pride, so I couldn't bring myself to say it.``
changeReason: NEW omits the final sentence (私のこと、許して下さいね / "Please, forgive me...") present in JPN and OLD.

TextENGNew: She turned a fawning smile my way, then gently placed a hand on my shoulder -- perhaps trying to ease the tension.
My body recoiled as though struck by lightning.
``Are you all right...?``
Her face showed concern, but something about Shion radiated a careless, desolate chill.
In desperation, I turned my eyes toward Rena.
And there... from the darkness, I saw Rena's silhouette pulling something from her duffel bag.
It was... a massive hatchet...!
Rena hoisted the hatchet onto her shoulder and raised it overhead, settling into a stance to await the target.
Both of Rena's eyes gleamed with a wet, red light...
Even Rena... had been hiding treachery behind an innocent face all along...?!
``...Rena-san feels the same way I do.@k@vS18/06/160600337. Though in her case, she also wants to confirm for herself whether that man's blood is truly red.``
Stripped of the great pillar of comrades' trust, I nearly lost consciousness as well.
My vision blurred, and Satoshi's bat -- slick with cold sweat -- threatened to slip from my grip.
Clinging to my last thread of composure, I focused on breathing.
...Haah... haah... haah...
There was no turning back now.
If I turned back... I'd be killed...
...Haah... haah... *retch*!!
I'd been so focused on breathing that I hyperventilated.
As I began to convulse, Shion's hand stroked my back.
``Uwaah!!``
I let out a sharp cry and crumpled to the ground.
Whether out of reflex or instinct, I expelled all the air I'd been holding in a single burst.
``...Uoowahhh...``
Shion remained perfectly calm as she rubbed my back.
``They say that if you breathe in too much oxygen, it becomes poison...@k@vS18/06/160600339. Isn't it frightening to think that something so ordinary around us can be toxic?``
``...Well, that's a relief at least...@k@vS18/06/160600341. If you'd thrown up, you would've left indisputable evidence at the crime scene...``
I just stood there, frozen.
I had finally let go of my sanity...
The only thing left was a pure, undiluted desire to kill...
......And then.
......The crickets fell silent.
...From far away, the sound of a motorcycle was drawing closer.
Desire and impulse surged up within me, and I felt every drop of blood in my body come to a boil.
Something took hold of me, driving my feet forward -- one step, then another.
Whether the entity possessing me was some supernatural being called Oyashiro-sama or not -- I no longer cared.
I had surrendered to the sweet temptation of being 'controlled,' and knelt before it.
...Closer and closer it came. ...The target was almost here.
Just a little farther... once he cleared those bushes ahead... he'd appear right in my line of sight.
...In that instant, I'd identify whether it was him... and if I confirmed it... if I confirmed it... my desire would reach its boiling point.
...I raised the bat overhead... and waited, single-mindedly, for him to draw his final breath... waited... waited...!!!
changeReason: OLD translates 事切れるまで as "waited for him to come out"; NEW correctly renders as "draw his final breath."

TextENGNew: The motorcycle came into view.
...Several unmistakable features whispered to me that this was indeed Teppei Houjou's bike...
...This was really... him...?
This wasn't a delusion or a hallucination...?
I checked the features one more time...
...The color, the shape... and above all... his clothes... his face...! That utterly rotten, utterly vile face!!
No mistake! No -- there was no possibility of a mistake!!
Closer, closer... the bike drew closer...
As if to nail my decision in place, two hands pressed against my back.
I didn't need to check who they belonged to. ...It was Oyashiro-sama.
I let my two gleaming eyes burn in the darkness, and a hazy thought drifted through my mind.
changeReason: OLD omits ぼんやりと思う ("a hazy thought occurred"), which sets up the philosophical reflection.

TextENGNew: The most terrifying thing in this world... was myself -- having lost the heart to believe, consumed by paranoia, reduced to nothing more than a beast.
Perhaps the entity called Oyashiro-sama, which had been gnawing at my heart with its fear and its authority, was me all along...
changeReason: OLD drops the revelation that Oyashiro-sama might be the narrator himself (俺自身だったのかもしれない).

TextENGNew: The motorcycle reached the target point...
``...I'll kill you...!!!!``
Teppei's motorcycle struck the rope stretched between Rena and Shion, and he lost his balance.
The instant Teppei's wretched scream tore through the air, I burst from the bushes.
*WHOOSH!!!*
Thrown suddenly to the ground, Teppei seemed unable to comprehend what had happened. He lay crumpled on the road, groaning.
Looking down at his exposed, pathetically defenseless form, I trembled -- not with fear, but with a smile surging up from within.
``...Heh... heh heh heh heh...@k@vS18/01/160100456. Aahahahahaha hahahaha hahahahahahaaaa!!!!``
I let out a laugh that could have filled the entire world, and brought the bat crashing down.
Teppei clutched his head reflexively and rolled about, unable to process the sudden, crushing pain.
I straddled him, cutting off any escape.
``Hey... do you know who I am...?``
Teppei blinked rapidly, squinting upward, trying to identify the figure looming over him.
``Uugh... my eyes... my eyyyyes!``
A sensation I had never experienced before filled me completely.
``Do you want to know who I am...?``
Without giving him a chance to answer, I swung the bat down again.
``Ugaaah... uooooohh!!``
Teppei writhed and screamed.
I mercilessly stomped on the hands he'd flung out in search of escape.
``My name... is Oyashiro-sama...``
I could feel Teppei being crushed under the weight of his terror.
When exposed to senseless, irrational terror, people cast aside their sanity.
I had already experienced that firsthand.
After that, it was nothing but frenzy.
I brought the bat down on Teppei's legs as he tried to crawl away in a wretched, shambling escape.
No matter how total the panic, human beings seem to possess an instinctive will to flee.
Teppei, even in his state, tried again and again to drag himself out from under me, desperately clawing at survival.
Teppei's face twisted further and further with terror. And the more he struggled to survive, the more a despair beyond all limits bore down on him.
``Tonight... is the night of the festivaaaal!!``
And then, to silence those grating sounds forever, to end that loathsome thrashing once and for all, I swung the bat down in a mindless frenzy, again and again.
``...Haah... haah... haah...``
Only my ragged breathing drifted into the pitch-black void.
I was alone in this vast, empty universe.
Teppei, the man I had hated so fiercely, was now just a symbol. Satoko, to whom I had pledged everything, was just a symbol. And the existence called Keiichi Maebara was itself just a symbol.
changeReason: Old misinterprets passage; Japanese shows everyone (including Keiichi himself) reduced to mere symbols.

TextENGNew: In this moment, I had become a murderer at the cost of losing everything I was...
changeReason: Old says 'without losing everything' but Japanese means 'at the cost of losing everything' -- reversed meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...Keiichi-kun...?``
Rena...?
Huh...? You were there...? ...Where are you, anyway?
Shion...?
I can only hear your voice. Show yourself... Are you hiding...?
Huh...? I can't see anything. Everything in front of me is pitch black... I can't see a thing...
I rubbed my eyes with one hand, and finally, blurred outlines began to take shape.
Rena and Shion stood there, staring at me...
Their outstretched hands hesitated to touch me...
changeReason: Old reverses subject: Japanese describes others' hands hesitating to touch narrator, not narrator hesitating.

TextENGNew: I happened to glance down at my palms. They glistened with something dark.
Is this... blood...?
At my feet lay Teppei's limp body.
Did I... kill him...?
I'd swung the bat again and again, relentlessly cornering him, beating him with all my strength!
That's right. You killed him... You've officially joined the ranks of murderers.
changeReason: OLD uses first person; JPN uses お前 (second person); NEW preserves the dissociative self-address.

TextENGNew: With exquisitely brutal savagery... you performed the main event of the Watanagashi -- the greatest spectacle of the festival!
changeReason: OLD significantly truncates, dropping the self-mocking spectacle description present in JPN.

TextENGNew: Laughter burst out of me like a broken dam.
Not because it was fun. Not because it was amusing. Not because I was happy. Not because I was proud.
I was... consumed by despair...
The bat slipped from between my fingers.
In that instant, I lost the very core of myself and collapsed to the ground.
``...Keiichi-kun, you're covered in blood...@k@vS18/02/160200321. Why, I wonder... it's pitch black...@k@vS18/02/160200322. So dark...``
Rena's murmured voice seemed to reach me from some distant world.
I gently closed my eyes and pressed my face against the cold ground, as if to confirm its reality.
In my hazy consciousness, I could hear Rena and Shion talking.
``...Is he really dead...?``
``Just to be certain...``
``Right... better safe than sorry...``
``...So he won't get back up...``
``...Rena will do it...``
``...So will I.``
I heard the sound of someone swallowing hard.
And then, an ear-splitting sound of destruction engulfed everything around me.
*THWACK!*
changeReason: OLD translates impact sound ドスッ as "whoosh" (movement); NEW correctly renders as "THWACK" (impact).

TextENGNew: *Rrring*...
The sounds repeated endlessly...
Raindrops began to patter against my cheek.
Before long, the rain intensified, summoning howling winds.
The festival was already over...
You arrived too late...
changeReason: OLD says "too late to go"; JPN addresses the storm directly ("You arrived too late"), which NEW captures.

TextENGNew: Storm, your cue has long since passed...
When I cracked my eyes open, a familiar shadow was looking down at me.
As my gaze slowly traveled upward, I saw...
Standing there was...
Is this some kind of joke...?
Right before my eyes... Satoko stood there, frozen in place...!!
``K-... Keiichi-san...!``
Sa... to... ko...
``...W-what are you doing...?!``
Yes -- until that very moment, I hadn't realized...
The fatal mistake we had made...
We'd been so fixated on 'the existence of Teppei Houjou' that we had completely overlooked the most critical thing of all -- 'Satoko Houjou's own intentions'...
Thunder roared like an explosion. ...The shockwave sent an electric current through my body.
I had lost control of both mind and body. I couldn't move.
All I could do was stare blankly at Satoko's face -- her eyes wide, her whole body trembling, her mind gone blank.
changeReason: OLD assigns trembling and wide eyes to Keiichi; JPN and NEW correctly attribute them to Satoko.

TextENGNew: The violent raindrops hammering my face seemed to wash away the tears that had traced down my cheeks without my knowing.
The storm had arrived at the most perfect timing.
But amid the raging storm, the one who let out a death cry was not Teppei -- it was Satoko.
``...Ugh, GYAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA!!!!``
That was the ending our festival reached...
A stirring finale............
The last thing I saw before my eyes closed...
Was... just like...
changeReason: OLD translates まるで as "only that"; NEW correctly renders as "just like" (simile setup).

TextENGNew: Just like............
changeReason: OLD translates まるで as "Only that"; NEW correctly renders as "Just like" (trailing simile).
